issues: panel occupies ~70% screen height, risks zoomed-in feel when open
lower grid rows mostly empty — sparse layout dilutes toy-diorama density
action sidebar (右侧按钮区) width inconsistent with panel visual language
reasoning: panel_inventory_v01 is a modal UI overlay; its ~480×480px footprint on a 1280×690 canvas (~37% W, ~70% H) is within expected range for inventory screens but pushes toward the large end for a toy-diorama aesthetic. Items inside each slot are consistently 50–70% of slot area — no item exceeds slot bounds, no item approaches player scale (32×32) in world-space. Icon sizes within the grid (~48–64px slots, icons ~32–40px) follow the ≤75% rule. Perspective is flat top-down pixel art, matching the visible game tiles behind the panel. No creature or effect scale errors present. Main concerns: (1) the panel height nearly fills the viewport, obscuring the game world and reducing the 'small figures in a diorama' sense; (2) bottom 2–3 grid rows are mostly empty outlines, making the panel feel underutilized and visually large; (3) right-side context buttons use a different visual style (flat green/blue rounded rects vs. pixel-art panel border), creating mild proportion incoherence. Overall functional and within spec but not tightly calibrated for toy-diorama density.
